Identification.LongTitle: An Act to implement the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia
Identification.ShortTitle: CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act
Identification.RunningHead: CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled âAn Act to implement the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesiaâ.
This enactment implements the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia, done at Ottawa on September 24, 2025. Among other things, it

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provides that no recourse may be taken on the basis of certain provisions of the enactment or any order made under those provisions, or on the basis of the provisions of the Agreement, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: provides for payment by Canada of its share of the expenditures associated with the operation of the institutional and administrative aspects of the Agreement; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: gives the Governor in Council the power to make orders in accordance with the Agreement.
It also makes related amendments to certain Acts to bring them into conformity with Canadaâs obligations under the Agreement.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to implement the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia

Text.XRefExternal: CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act

TableOfProvisions.Text: Non-application of Act and Agreement to water

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Agreementâmeans the Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia, done at Ottawa on September 24, 2025.â(Accord)
Committeeâmeans the Joint Committee established under Article 23.1 of the Agreement.â(ComitÃ©)

federal lawâmeans the whole or any portion of an Act of Parliament or a regulation, order or other instrument issued, made or established in the exercise of a power conferred by or under an Act of Parliament.â(texte lÃ©gislatif fÃ©dÃ©ral)
Ministerâmeans the Minister for International Trade.â(ministre)

Section.Text: For greater certainty, this Act and any federal law that implements a provision of the Agreement or that allows the Government of Canada to meet an obligation under the Agreement is to be interpreted in a manner consistent with the Agreement.

Section.MarginalNote: Non-application of Act and Agreement to water
Section.Text: For greater certainty, nothing in this Act or the Agreement applies to natural surface or ground water in liquid, gaseous or solid state.

For greater certainty, nothing in this Act, by specific mention or omission, is to be construed to affect in any manner the right of Parliament to enact legislation that implements any provision of the Agreement or that allows the Government of Canada to meet an obligation under the Agreement.
This Act is binding on His Majesty in right of Canada.
The purpose of this Act is to implement the Agreement, the objectives of which, as elaborated more specifically through its provisions, are to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: establish a free trade area in accordance with the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: promote, through the expansion of reciprocal trade, the harmonious development of economic relations between Canada and Indonesia in order to create opportunities for economic growth;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: provide for a predictable framework for business planning and investment;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: promote fair competition in trade between Canada and Indonesia;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: substantially increase investment opportunities in Canada and Indonesia, while preserving the right of each party to the Agreement to regulate to achieve legitimate policy goals;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: reduce or eliminate barriers to trade in goods and services in order to contribute to the harmonious development and expansion of trade;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: seek to ensure that the benefits and opportunities created by the Agreement are widely shared;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: support the growth and development of micro, small and medium-sized enterprises by enhancing their ability to participate in and benefit from the opportunities created by the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: promote environmental protection, including through effective enforcement of environmental laws, mutually supportive trade and environmental policies and practices, and strengthened environmental cooperation between Canada and Indonesia;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: promote the protection and enforcement of labour rights and the improvement of working conditions;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: encourage enterprises operating within Canada or Indonesia or subject to their jurisdiction to respect internationally recognized corporate social responsibility and responsible business conduct standards and principles;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: promote sustainable development;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: promote transparency, good governance and the rule of law, while strengthening commitments to combat bribery and corruption in trade and investment;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: recognize the importance of increased engagement by Indigenous Peoples in trade and investment and facilitate such engagement; and

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: recognize the right of the parties to the Agreement to adopt or maintain measures with respect to cultural industries in accordance with the rights and obligations provided for in the Agreement.

Subsection.Text: No person has any cause of action and no proceedings of any kind are to be taken, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada, to enforce or determine any right or obligation that is claimed or arises solely under or by virtue of sections 11 to 14 or an order made under section 15.

Subject to Articles 10.24 and 13.22 to 13.43 of the Agreement, no person has any cause of action and no proceedings of any kind are to be taken, without the consent of the Attorney General of Canada, to enforce or determine any right or obligation that is claimed or arises solely under or by virtue of the Agreement.

Section.Text: The Agreement is approved.
The Minister is the principal representative of Canada on the Committee.
The Government of Canada is to pay its appropriate share of the aggregate of any expenditures incurred by or on behalf of the Committee.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to subsections (2) to (4), appoint representatives of Canada to any panel, committee, subcommittee, working group, expert group or other subsidiary body referred to in Chapter 23 or 24 of the Agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: appoint a panellist in accordance with Article 24.8 of the Agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: propose candidates to serve as the chair of a panel, or select the chair, in accordance with that Agreement.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Power of Minister of the Environment
Subsection.Text: The Minister of the Environment may, with the concurrence of the Minister, appoint representatives of Canada to the Environment Sub-Committee established under Article 17.21 of the Agreement.
The Minister of Labour may, with the concurrence of the Minister, appoint representatives of Canada to the Labour Council established under Article 17.32 of the Agreement.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Power of Minister â Article 17.47 of Agreement
Subsection.Text: The Minister may, with the concurrence of the Minister of the Environment and the Minister of Labour, appoint representatives of Canada to the Committee on Trade and Sustainable Development established under Article 17.47 of the Agreement.
The Minister may delegate the Ministerâs powers, duties or functions under any of subsections (1) to (4) to any officer of the Department of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development.

Section.Text: The Minister is to designate an agency, division or branch of the Government of Canada to facilitate the operation of Chapter 24 of the Agreement and to provide administrative assistance to panels referred to in that Chapter.
The Government of Canada is to pay the costs of or its appropriate share of the costs of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the remuneration and expenses payable to members of panels, committees, subcommittees, working groups, expert groups and other subsidiary bodies, to independent experts and to the assistants of panel members; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the general expenses incurred by panels, committees, subcommittees, working groups, expert groups and other subsidiary bodies.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, for the purpose of suspending benefits in accordance with Article 24.13 of the Agreement, by order, do any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend rights or privileges granted by Canada under the Agreement or any federal law to Indonesia, in respect of goods of Indonesia, to service suppliers or investors of Indonesia, or in respect of investments of investors of Indonesia;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: modify or suspend the application of any federal law in respect of Indonesia, goods of Indonesia, service suppliers or investors of Indonesia or investments of investors of Indonesia;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: extend the application of any federal law to Indonesia, goods of Indonesia, service suppliers or investors of Indonesia or investments of investors of Indonesia;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: take any other measure that the Governor in Council considers necessary.

Subsection.Text: Unless repealed, an order made under subsection (1) has effect for the period specified in the order.

Section.Text: Schedule VII to the Financial Administration Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Item.Text: Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia, done at Ottawa on September 24, 2025.

Section.Text: The schedule to the Investment Canada Act is amended by adding, at the end of column 1, a reference to âAgreement within the meaning of section 2 of the CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Actâ and a corresponding reference to âArticle 1.5â in column 2.
Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: CICEPAâhas the same meaning as Agreement in section 2 of the CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act.â(APÃGCI)
Indonesiaâhas the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff.â(IndonÃ©sie)

Section.Text: Subsection 35.1(3.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Certificate of origin completed by importer
Subsection.Text: If an importer of goods for which preferential tariff treatment under the CPTPP, CUSMA or CICEPA will be claimed is the person who certifies that the goods meet the rules of origin set out in, or contemplated by, the CPTPP, CUSMA or CICEPA, the importer shall do so in writing, in the prescribed form with the prescribed information, and on the basis of supporting documents that the importer has or supporting documents that are provided by the exporter or producer.
Subsection 97.1(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Certificate of Origin â CPTPP, CUSMA or CICEPA
Subsection.Text: If an exporter or producer of goods that are exported to a CPTPP country, a CUSMA country or Indonesia and for which preferential tariff treatment under the CPTPP, CUSMA or CICEPA will be claimed in accordance with the laws of that country is the person who certifies that the goods meet the rules of origin set out in, or contemplated by, the CPTPP, CUSMA or CICEPA, the exporter or producer shall do so in writing, in the prescribed form with the prescribed information, and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an exporter, on the basis of supporting information that the exporter has or by relying on supporting information that the producer has; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a producer, on the basis of supporting information that the producer has.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 97.1(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

A person who has completed and signed a certificate in accordance with subsection (1) or (1.1) and who has reason to believe that it contains incorrect information shall immediately notify each person and each country or authority to whom the certificate was given of the correct information.

Section.Text: Part 1 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âIndonesiaâ in column 1, a corresponding reference to âCICEPAâ in column 2 and a corresponding reference to âIndonesia Tariff rates of customs duty under the Customs Tariffâ in column 3.
Part 2 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCICEPAâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âparagraph 4 of Article 3.25â in column 2.

Part 3 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âIndonesiaâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âsubparagraphs 1(a), (c) and (d) of Article 4.8 of CICEPAâ in column 2.
Part 4 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âIndonesiaâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âCICEPAâ in column 2.
Part 5 of the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âCICEPAâ in column 1 and a corresponding reference to âChapters 3 and 4â in column 2.

Schedule 2 to the Commercial Arbitration Act is amended by adding, at the end of column 1, a reference to âArticle 13.25â and a corresponding reference to âComprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement between Canada and Indonesia, done at Ottawa on September 24, 2025â in column 2.

Subsection.Text: Section 2 of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4.5):
In this Act, Indonesia Tariff means the rates of customs duty referred to in section 49.71 of the Customs Tariff.
Subsection 2(5) of the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to âIndonesiaâ in the list of countries.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 19.0191:

Subsection.Text: In this section, principal cause means, in respect of a serious injury or threat of a serious injury, an important cause that is no less important than any other cause of the serious injury or threat.

The Tribunal shall inquire into and report to the Governor in Council on the question whether goods that are entitled to the benefit of the Indonesia Tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, if the Governor in Council, on the recommendation of the Minister, refers the question to it for an inquiry and report.
The Tribunal shall conduct an inquiry under subsection (2) and prepare its report in accordance with the terms of reference established by the Governor in Council or the Minister, as the case may be.

The Minister shall cause a copy of each report submitted to the Governor in Council or the Minister to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the report is so submitted.
The Tribunal shall cause notice of the submission of a report to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 20.07:

Subsection.Text: In this section, principal cause means, in respect of a serious injury or threat of a serious injury, an important cause that is no less important than any other cause of the serious injury or threat.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Determination in respect of goods imported from Indonesia
Subsection.Text: If, in an inquiry conducted under section 20 into goods imported from Indonesia that are specified by the Governor in Council or in an inquiry conducted pursuant to a complaint under subsection 23(1) into goods so imported that are specified by the Tribunal, the Tribunal finds that the specified imported goods and goods of the same kind imported from other countries are being imported in such increased quantities and under such conditions as to be a principal cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, the Tribunal shall determine whether the specified imported goods are a principal cause of the serious injury or threat of serious injury.
In the case of an inquiry to which subsection (2) applies, the Tribunal shall include in its report any determinations made under that subsection.

In an inquiry under section 30.07 into goods imported from Indonesia conducted pursuant to an extension request, the Tribunal shall determine whether the goods imported from Indonesia are a principal cause of the serious injury or threat of serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods.

Section.Text: Section 21.1 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In sections 23 to 30, complaint means a written complaint filed with the Tribunal under any of subsections 23(1) to (1.098) and, for the purposes of those sections, a complaint is properly documented if the Tribunal is satisfied that it contains or is accompanied by the information required by section 23.
Section 23 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.097):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Filing of complaint â Indonesia Tariff
Subsection.Text: Any domestic producer of goods that are like or directly competitive with any goods being imported into Canada and that are entitled to the Indonesia Tariff, or any person or association acting on or behalf of such a domestic producer, may file a written complaint with the Tribunal alleging that, as a result of that entitlement, the imported goods are being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods.

Section.Text: Paragraph 25(2)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.06), (1.08), (1.083), (1.09), (1.091), (1.092), (1.096) or (1.098), send to the Minister a copy of the complaint and the information examined by the Tribunal in making its determination.

Section.Text: Paragraph 26(1)(a) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (i.97):

Subparagraph.Label: (i.98)
Subparagraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.098), the goods that are entitled to the Indonesia Tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods,

Section.Text: Subsection 27(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (a.97):

Paragraph.Label: (a.98)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.098), the goods that are entitled to the Indonesia Tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause of serious injury, or threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 30.03(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

The Tribunal shall cause to be published in the Canada Gazette a notice of the expiry date of any order that imposes a surtax on any goods under subsection 55(1), section 60, subsection 63(1), 74(1) or (2), 75(1), 76(1) or 77(1) of the Customs Tariff or includes any goods on the Import Control List under subsection 5(3), (3.2) or (4.1) of the Export and Import Permits Act, but no notice shall be published if
Subsection 30.03(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (d), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the effective period specified in the order and any periods during which the goods were subject to a surtax imposed by an order made under subsection 75(1) of the Customs Tariff is three years.

Section.Text: Subsection 30.04(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Filing of request relating to extension orders

Subsection.Text: Any domestic producer of goods that are like or directly competitive with any goods that are subject to an order referred to in subsection 30.03(1), or any person or association acting on behalf of any such domestic producer, may file with the Tribunal a written request that an extension order be made under subsection 63(1), 74(7), 75(3), 76(3) or 77(3) of the Customs Tariff or subsection 5(3.2) of the Export and Import Permits Act because an order continues to be necessary to prevent or remedy serious injury to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods.

Section.Text: Subsection 2(1) of the Customs Tariff is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreementâhas the same meaning as Agreement in section 2 of the CanadaâIndonesia Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Implementation Act.â(Accord de partenariat Ã©conomique global CanadaâIndonÃ©sie)

Indonesiaâmeans the land territories, internal waters, archipelagic waters, territorial sea, including the seabed and subsoil thereof, and air space over such territories and waters, as well as the contiguous zone, the continental shelf and exclusive economic zone, over which Indonesia has sovereignty, sovereign rights or jurisdiction as defined in its laws, and in accordance with international law, including the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, done at Montego Bay on December 10, 1982.â(IndonÃ©sie)

Section.Text: Paragraph 14(2)(c) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (xv):

Subparagraph.Label: (xvi)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 75(1);

Section.Text: Section 27 of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 49.7:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 24, goods that originate in Indonesia are entitled to the Indonesia Tariff rates of customs duty.

If âAâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âIDTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Indonesia Tariff, the Indonesia Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the final rate of âFreeâ.

If âFâ is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âIDTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Indonesia Tariff, the Indonesia Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced as provided in the âFâ Staging List.

If âY1â, âY2â or âY3â is set out in the column entitled âPreferential Tariffâ in the List of Tariff Provisions following the abbreviation âIDTâ in relation to goods entitled to the Indonesia Tariff, the Indonesia Tariff rate of customs duty that applies to those goods is the initial rate, reduced

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if âY1â is set out,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on the coming into force of this subsection, to four fifths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is one year after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to three fifths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is two years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to two fifths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is three years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to the one fifth of the initial rate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is four years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to the final rate of âFreeâ;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if âY2â is set out,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on the coming into force of this subsection, to nine tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is one year after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to eight tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is two years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to seven tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is three years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to six tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is four years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to five tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is five years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to four tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is six years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to three tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is seven years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to two tenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is eight years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to one tenth of the initial rate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is nine years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to the final rate of âFreeâ; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if âY3â is set out,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on the coming into force of this subsection, to fourteen fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is one year after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to thirteen fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is two years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to twelve fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is three years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to eleven fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is four years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to ten fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is five years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to nine fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is six years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to eight fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is seven years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to seven fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is eight years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to six fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is nine years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to five fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is 10 years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to four fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (xii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is 11 years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to three fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (xiii)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is 12 years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to two fifteenths of the initial rate,

Subparagraph.Label: (xiv)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is 13 years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to one fifteenth of the initial rate, and

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: effective on January 1 of the year that is 14 years after the year of the coming into force of this subsection, to the final rate of âFreeâ.

Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a specific rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one tenth of a cent, the rate shall be rounded down to the nearest one tenth of a cent.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rounding of amounts â fraction other than 0.5
Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that includes a fraction of one per cent other than 0.5, the resulting percentage shall be rounded down to the nearest percentage that divides evenly by 0.5.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Elimination of rates of less than two per cent
Subsection.Text: If a reduction under subsection (3) or (4) results in a rate of customs duty that is a percentage of less than two per cent, the rate shall be further reduced to âFreeâ immediately.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 74:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (2) to (5), if at any time it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, as a result of an inquiry made by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal under subsection 19.0192(2) of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act or further to a complaint filed under subsection 23(1.098) of that Act, that goods that are entitled to the Indonesia Tariff are, as a result of that entitlement, being imported in such increased quantities, in absolute terms or relative to the domestic production of like or directly competitive goods, and under such conditions as to constitute a principal cause of serious injury, or a threat of serious injury, to domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods, the Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: suspend, during the period that the order is in effect, any reduction of the rate of customs duty with respect to those goods that would otherwise be made after that time by virtue of section 49.71; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: make those goods subject to a temporary duty, in addition to any other duty specified in this Act or in any other Act of Parliament relating to customs, at a rate set out in the order, but that rate, when added to the rate of customs duty specified in the Indonesia Tariff that is in effect in respect of those goods at that time, may not exceed the lesser of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that is in effect in respect of those goods at the time the order is made, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Most-Favoured-Nation Tariff rate of customs duty that was in effect in respect of those goods immediately before the coming into force of this subsection.

Subsection.Text: An order under subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is not to be made more than once in respect of goods of any particular kind;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: remains in effect only for the period, not exceeding two years, that is specified in the order;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: may be made during the period beginning on the day on which this subsection comes into force and ending

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the order is in respect of goods for which the Indonesia Tariff rate of customs duty is reduced to the final rate of âFreeâ over a period of less than 10 years, on the day that is 10 years after the day on which this subsection comes into force, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the order is in respect of goods for which the Indonesia Tariff rate of customs duty is reduced to the final rate of âFreeâ over a period of 10 years or more, on the day after the expiry of the tariff staging period in respect of those goods.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister, by order, extend the period of an order made under subsection (1), if it appears to the satisfaction of the Governor in Council, as a result of an inquiry made by the Canadian International Trade Tribunal under section 30.07 of the Canadian International Trade Tribunal Act that the order continues to be necessary to prevent or remedy serious injury to, and to facilitate the adjustment of, domestic producers of like or directly competitive goods. The total period of the order is not to exceed three years.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Rate of duty when order ceases to have effect
Subsection.Text: If an order made under subsection (1) ceases to have effect in a particular calendar year, the rate of customs duty applicable to the goods after the order ceases to have effect is the rate of customs duty that is applicable in accordance with section 49.71.
In this section, principal cause means, in respect of a serious injury or threat of serious injury, an important cause that is not less important than any other cause of the serious injury or threat.

Section.Text: Section 79 of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (o):

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 75(1).

Section.Text: The List of Countries and Applicable Tariff Treatments set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by adding, in the column âTariff Treatment / Otherâ, a reference to âIDTâ opposite the reference to âIndonesiaâ.
The List of Tariff Provisions set out in the schedule to the Act is amended by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the column âPreferential Tariff / Initial Rateâ, above the reference to âGPTâ, a reference to âIDT:â;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the column âPreferential Tariff / Final Rateâ, above the reference to âGPTâ, a reference to âIDT:â;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the column âPreferential Tariff / Initial Rateâ a reference to âFreeâ after the abbreviation âIDTâ, and adding in the column âPreferential Tariff / Final Rateâ a reference to âFree (A)â after the abbreviation âIDTâ, for all tariff items except those tariff items set out in Schedules 1 and 2 to this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the columns âPreferential Tariff / Initial Rateâ and âPreferential Tariff / Final Rateâ, a reference to âN/Aâ after the abbreviation âIDTâ for those tariff items set out in Schedule 1 to this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: adding in the columns âPreferential Tariff / Initial Rateâ and âPreferential Tariff / Final Rateâ after the abbreviation âIDTâ, for each tariff item set out in Schedule 2 to this Act, the rates of duty and staging categories set out with respect to that tariff item in that Schedule.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
